expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads deployedContracts from SE-2 nextjs package
expectations.evidence: ponder.config.ts line 2: import deployedContracts from "../nextjs/contracts/deployedContracts"; — correctly imports from the SE-2 nextjs package and uses it to build contract configs with ABI, address, and startBlock.

expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads scaffoldConfig for network detection
expectations.evidence: ponder.config.ts line 3: import scaffoldConfig from "../nextjs/scaffold.config"; — uses scaffoldConfig.targetNetworks[0] to determine the target network for chain and contract configuration.

expectations.text: Package named @se-2/ponder following SE-2 workspace convention
expectations.evidence: packages--ponder--package.json line 2: "name": "@se-2/ponder" — follows the @se-2/ workspace naming convention. Root package.json also includes "packages/ponder" in workspaces.

expectations.text: Uses Ponder virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api)
expectations.evidence: YourContract.ts uses: import { ponder } from "ponder:registry" and import { greetingChange } from "ponder:schema". api/index.ts uses: import { db } from "ponder:api" and import schema from "ponder:schema". All three virtual modules are used.

expectations.text: Schema uses onchainTable (not older createSchema API)
expectations.evidence: ponder.schema.ts line 1: import { onchainTable } from "ponder"; — uses onchainTable to define the greetingChange table with typed columns (text, hex, boolean, bigint, integer).
YourContract.ts line 4: ponder.on("YourContract:GreetingChange", ...) — uses the correct ContractName:EventName format.
YourContract.ts line 5: await context.db.insert(greetingChange).values({ id: event.id, greetingSetter: event.args.greetingSetter, ... }); — uses the correct Drizzle-style insert pattern.

expectations.text: Hono-based API setup for GraphQL (not old express-style)
expectations.evidence: api/index.ts: imports Hono from "hono", creates const app = new Hono(), and sets up app.use("/graphql", graphql({ db, schema })). Exports default app. This is the correct modern Hono-based pattern.

expectations.text: Root package.json has ponder proxy scripts
expectations.evidence: Root package.json includes multiple ponder proxy scripts: ponder:dev, ponder:start, ponder:codegen, ponder:serve, ponder:lint, ponder:typecheck — all using "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder" pattern.

ponder-env.d.ts exists with 51 lines declaring modules for ponder:registry, ponder:schema, and ponder:api with full type definitions including Virtual.Registry, Virtual.Schema, Virtual.Drizzle, and related types.

eval_feedback.overall: All 10 expectations pass. The Ponder integration is comprehensive and well-structured: ponder.config.ts correctly reads both deployedContracts and scaffoldConfig from the SE-2 nextjs package, the package follows workspace naming conventions, all modern Ponder APIs are used (onchainTable, virtual module imports, Drizzle-style db writes, Hono-based API), the root package.json has proper proxy scripts, and the type declaration file is complete.
